---
title: "Example: Negative Binomial paired nested bootstrap CV (template)"
format: html
execute:
  echo: true
  warning: false
  message: false
---

# Load generic functions
source("R/cvb_nb_paired.R")

set.seed(1)

# Toy count data (NB-like)
n <- 60
df <- data.frame(
  x1 = rnorm(n),
  x2 = rnorm(n)
)

# Create a mean structure and generate NB counts
mu <- exp(0.2 + 0.6*df$x1 - 0.4*df$x2)
theta <- 2
df$y <- rnbinom(n, mu = mu, size = theta)

# Candidate models
model_formulas <- list(
  "Model 1" = y ~ x1,
  "Model 2" = y ~ x2,
  "Model 3" = y ~ x1 + x2,
  "Model 4" = y ~ 1
)

# Run (small B for demo; increase in real use)
res <- cvb_nb_single_nestedLOO_paired(
  data = df,
  model_formulas = model_formulas,
  region_label = "toy_nb",
  outcome_var = "y",
  B = 50,
  seed = 123,
  out_dir = "analysis/_demo_outputs"
)

res$rds_path
res$summary_raw

# Build reporting outputs from the saved RDS (source of truth)
make_table_312(
  rds_path = res$rds_path,
  region_label = "toy_nb",
  out_csv = "Table_3_1_2_toy_nb.csv",
  out_dir = "analysis/_demo_outputs"
)

make_table_313(
  rds_path = res$rds_path,
  region_label = "toy_nb",
  out_csv = "Table_3_1_3_toy_nb.csv",
  out_dir = "analysis/_demo_outputs"
)

# Preview
read.csv("analysis/_demo_outputs/Table_3_1_2_toy_nb.csv")
head(read.csv("analysis/_demo_outputs/Table_3_1_3_toy_nb.csv"))
